The project aligns with India's sustainability goals by cutting operational emissions.

tech.headline: 94-MW Moraine Solar project adopts Piled QA/QC platform

The project, awarded by AES, involves over 77,000 pilings and focuses on quality assurance and control during foundation installation.

tech.headline: Coyote Valley Casino Finishes 2.23-MW Solar Carport
tech.summary: Coyote Valley Casino & Hotel in Redwood Valley, California, completed a 2.23-MW solar carport system developed by Watthub Renewables and constructed by SunRenu Solar. The project includes a PPA with Sunrock Distributed Generation, requiring no upfront capital from the Coyote Valley Band of Pomo Indians.

tech.headline: PowerBank Brings Online 7-MW NY Community Solar Project
tech.summary: PowerBank completed the SB 13-2 7-MWDC community solar project in upstate New York for Honeywell International. Built on an industrial brownfield regulated by the New York State Dept. of Environmental Conservation, it is part of a three-project portfolio developed by PowerBank for Honeywell.

learn.title: Flow Batteries for Grid Storage
learn.intro: Flow batteries use liquid electrolytes to store electricity, offering scalable, long-duration storage for renewable grids. Their ability to decouple power and energy capacity makes them ideal for balancing solar and wind variability, reducing curtailment, and supporting grid reliability as the energy transition accelerates.
learn.c1_text: Think of a giant water tank: one side stores the liquid, the other pumps it through a turbine. Flow batteries circulate vanadium or iron electrolytes, allowing unlimited energy capacity by simply enlarging the tanks, much like adding more water to a reservoir.
learn.c2_text: Vanadium cells achieve 80% round‑trip efficiency and can cycle thousands of times, while iron‑sulfur batteries are cheaper but have lower energy density. Both excel in 10‑hour storage, ideal for day‑to‑night solar balancing.
learn.c3_text: India’s Andhra Pradesh grid uses a 20 MW/80 MWh vanadium flow battery to smooth solar output, reducing peak loads by 15%. The project demonstrates flow batteries’ role in India’s 175 GW renewable target by 2025.
learn.stat2: US DOE: 7 MW flow battery pilot in Washington (2023)
learn.stat3: Global flow battery market projected to reach $3.5 B by 2028
learn.stat4: Vanadium flow batteries achieve 80% round‑trip efficiency

learn.title: Smart Grids & Advanced Metering
learn.intro: Smart grids digitize electricity networks, enabling real‑time monitoring, automated control, and consumer participation. Advanced metering transforms flat rate bills into dynamic pricing, encouraging energy use when renewables are abundant and reducing peak demand, thus accelerating the clean energy transition.
learn.c1_text: Imagine a city’s traffic lights that communicate with each other. Smart grids use sensors, IoT devices, and AI to manage power flow, preventing blackouts and optimizing renewable integration, just like traffic lights reduce congestion.
learn.c2_text: AMI delivers two‑way data between utilities and homes, enabling real‑time pricing signals. A 10% price increase during peak hours can shift 5 % of residential demand, easing strain on the grid.
learn.c3_text: India’s Smart Metering Initiative plans 30 million meters by 2025, aiming to cut peak demand by 2 GW. The project will empower consumers to use solar PV during sunny periods, boosting rooftop adoption.
learn.stat2: IEA: Smart grids could reduce global carbon emissions by 1.3 GtCO₂e by 2030
learn.stat3: India plans 30 M smart meters by 2025
learn.stat4: Smart meters cut peak demand by 2 GW in pilot regions

learn.title: Lithium‑Ion Battery Storage
learn.intro: Lithium‑ion batteries store excess renewable electricity, providing instant power for grid balancing and vehicle propulsion. Their high energy density and rapid response make them essential for peak shaving, frequency regulation, and decarbonizing transportation, driving the clean energy transition forward.
learn.c1_text: Picture a rechargeable toy: lithium ions shuttle between two electrodes during charge and discharge, releasing electrons that power devices. The process repeats thousands of times, similar to a rechargeable battery in a smartphone.
learn.c2_text: Large lithium‑ion plants store 100‑MW/400‑MWh of energy, delivering grid services like frequency regulation in 5 seconds and peak shaving of 10 MW, reducing fossil‑fuel curtailment.
learn.c3_text: India’s 200 MW/800 MWh lithium‑ion plant in Tamil Nadu supports 30 GW of solar, preventing 1.2 MtCO₂e annual emissions and showcasing storage’s role in India’s 175 GW renewable goal.
learn.stat2: US DOE: 200 MW/800 MWh lithium‑ion storage pilot (2023)

learn.title: Floating Solar Farms
learn.intro: Floating solar farms place PV panels on lakes, reservoirs, or seas, saving land and cooling modules through water contact. They reduce evaporation, preserve freshwater, and can power coastal communities, making them a win‑win for renewable generation and water conservation in the energy transition.
learn.c1_text: Like a floating greenhouse, panels sit above water, keeping them cooler and reducing electricity loss by ~10%. They also protect water surfaces, cutting evaporation by up to 30%, vital for water‑scarce regions.
learn.c2_text: Floating arrays use pontoons, mooring lines, and corrosion‑resistant materials. A 100 MW plant on a 5‑ha lake can generate 200 GWh/year, equivalent to powering 40,000 homes.
learn.c3_text: India’s 20 MW floating solar at Sardar Sarovar Lake supplies 40,000 households and reduces evaporation by 15%, aligning with the country’s 450 GW renewable target by 2030.
learn.stat2: IRENA: 200 MW floating plant reduces evaporation 30%
learn.stat4: Floating solar efficiency rises 5–10% due to cooling

learn.title: Hydrogen Fuel Cells
learn.intro: Hydrogen fuel cells generate electricity by combining hydrogen with oxygen, producing only water vapor. They enable zero‑emission vehicles, power backup systems, and industrial processes, making hydrogen a cornerstone of a decarbonized transportation and energy sector.
learn.c1_text: Think of a battery that keeps recharging itself: hydrogen atoms split into protons and electrons, electrons flow through a circuit to produce power, while protons pass through a membrane, joining oxygen to form water.
learn.c2_text: A 1‑MW fuel cell can power a bus for 500 km on a single 5 kg hydrogen tank, offering 70% of the energy density of gasoline while emitting only water vapor.
learn.c3_text: India’s National Hydrogen Mission targets 5 Mt of green hydrogen by 2030, with 10 MW pilot plants in Gujarat, aiming to power 100,000 vehicles and reduce urban air pollution.
learn.stat2: US DOE: 1 MW fuel cell bus range 500 km
learn.stat3: India’s mission: 5 Mt green hydrogen by 2030

learn.title: Sodium‑Ion Batteries
learn.intro: Sodium‑ion batteries use abundant sodium instead of scarce lithium, offering a lower‑cost, scalable alternative for grid storage. Their comparable performance, coupled with global sodium availability, positions them as a key technology for affordable, large‑scale renewable integration worldwide.
learn.c1_text: Sodium ions move between electrodes just like lithium ions, but sodium’s larger size reduces energy density to ~120 Wh/kg, still enough for 10‑hour storage in 1‑MW plants.
learn.c2_text: Sodium is 200× more abundant than lithium, reducing raw material costs by 40%. Pilot plants in China demonstrate 200 kWh storage at $200/kWh, half the price of lithium‑ion.
learn.c3_text: India’s 50 MW/200 MWh sodium‑ion plant in Rajasthan will support 20 GW of solar, cutting storage costs by 30% and aligning with the 175 GW renewable target by 2025.
learn.stat3: India’s 50 MW plant supports 20 GW solar
